Our Projects

Where ideas meet the ground

From masterplans to community forums, our projects turn research and dialogue into real steps toward fairer, more sustainable Indonesian cities.

40+Cities & regions engaged
15+Partner institutions
100+Program participants
2023Working since
Featured Project

Signage & Wayfinding for Surabaya

A multi-stakeholder engagement to reshape how people navigate Surabaya — running an engagement day, workshop, and focus group discussion with the Ministry of Transportation, UK PACT, WRI, ARUP, and Transport for Surabaya.

LocationSurabaya
Year2025
RoleResearch & Facilitation
Advisory
Madiun

Planologic Contributes to I-PRO Documents for Madiun Regency

Supporting the preparation of the Madiun Regency I-PRO (Regional Investment Opportunity Profile) documents.

2025
Research
Madiun

Madiun Regency Industrial Estate

Partnered with the ITS team to help draft the masterplan for an industrial area designated as a National Strategic Project (PSN).

2025
Research
Indonesia

Rethinking Urban Growth: Addressing Urban Sprawl in Indonesia

A study on urban sprawl and equitable regional development, in collaboration with Bappenas.

2025
Discussion
Jakarta

Planologic Discusses Palestine Through the Lens of Urban Planning

“Kolaborasi Inspirasi” — a public discussion on spatial planning, conflict, and humanity in Palestine, in collaboration with Dompet Dhuafa.

2025
Community
Jakarta

Stand Up for Clean Air

Joining the call for cleaner air in Indonesian cities through advocacy and community engagement.

2023
Community
Surabaya

Urban Issues Through Women’s Lens

A community discussion forum exploring gender perspectives in the city — how public space and planning affect women’s daily lives.

2023
Learning
Various

Exploring Active Mobility and the 15-Minute City

Exploring active mobility and proximity-based planning concepts to strengthen research and planning capacity.

2024
Media
Yogyakarta

Inspirasi Nusantara on TVRI

Our co-founder featured on TVRI’s Inspirasi Nusantara, discussing how young people can help build better Indonesian cities.
